title: Work permits for permanent residence applicants - Canada.ca
content:
## Work permits for permanent residence applicants
Apply for a work permit that lets you or your family members work while you wait for a decision on your permanent residence.
- Sign in to your account
- Check our current processing times
- Check your application status
- Find a job on Job Bank

## Services and information

### Bridging open work permits (BOWPs)
For permanent residence applicants (including Express Entry, provincial nominees) with an expiring work permit
Open work permit for a sponsored spouse or common-law partner and accompanying children

### Home Child Care Provider Pilot and Home Support Worker Pilot Closed
Open work permit to meet the 12-month work experience requirement in the Gaining experience category

### Temporary resident (TR) to permanent resident (PR) pathway
Open work permits for you and your family members

### Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP)
2-year work permit to work for the employer who offered you the job
2-year open work permit to start setting up your business while you wait for a decision

### Quebec-selected skilled workers
Work permits for Quebec Selection Certificate (CSQ) holders

### Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP)
1-year work permit to work for the employer who offered you the job in the participating community

### Rural Community Immigration Pilot (RCIP)
2-year work permit to work for the employer who offered you the job in the participating community

### Francophone Community Immigration Pilot (FCIP)
2-year work permit to work for the employer who offered you the job in the participating community

### Agri-Food Pilot (AFP)
Open work permits for your family depending on your TEER category

### Yukon Community Pilot Closed
Location-specific work permit for Yukon nominees under the provincial nominee program.
3-year open work permit to work while you wait for a decision

### Medical doctors and certain healthcare specialists
Faster processing for your employer-specific work permit

### Explore immigration programs
Answer a few questions to see different ways you might be able to come to Canada

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
